scenario: Aurora Bank models a customer onboarding process in Camunda 8.8 that handles 8000 customers per month. The BPMN file has only one starting point and the process is launched via a single upstream API channel for every new applicant. The team must pick the start event symbol that fits a clearly standard entry point with one trigger source.
question: Which BPMN start event symbol fits a process definition that has only one clearly standard starting point?

options.text: Use a none start event; it indicates the typical starting point and only one such start event can exist in a single process definition.
Use a message start event; it differentiates several start events whenever a process has more than one distinct trigger source per definition.
Use a timer start event; it automatically starts process instances on a time controlled schedule rather than from a single upstream API call.
Use a signal start event; it starts several process instances at once whenever a broadcast signal arrives at the workflow engine.

a.text: Correct. The best practice table marks the none start event as the choice when you have only one start event or one which is clearly standard. The page also notes that only one such start event can exist in a process definition, which matches the single upstream API channel described for the onboarding flow.

b.text: Incorrect. The message start event is the choice when you have to differentiate several start events in one process. With only one clearly standard entry point on the BPMN file, the table points to the none start event instead.

c.text: Incorrect. The timer start event is reserved for automatically starting instances time controlled, not for a single API triggered onboarding. The standard entry point in the scenario is the none start event according to the table.

d.text: Incorrect. The signal start event is reserved for the rare case of starting several process instances at once via a broadcast. A single upstream API call to one standard entry point matches the none start event row of the table.

explanation: The routing events best practice lists the none start event as the choice when there is only one clearly standard starting point and notes that only one such event can exist in a process definition.
